name: Tiny Takeover
summary: All baby mobs get completely redesigned with unique textures and sounds instead of just being shrunken adults. Golden dandelions keep baby mobs young forever, and name tags are now craftable.
details: Baby Mob Overhaul — every baby mob gets unique textures and sounds instead of being a scaled-down adult
Golden Dandelion — a rare flower that keeps baby mobs young forever when nearby
Craftable Name Tags — name tags can finally be crafted instead of only found as loot
Unique baby animations — each baby mob type has distinct behaviors and movement styles

name: Mounts of Mayhem
summary: The nautilus becomes the first rideable underwater mob, spears deal damage based on player speed, and zombie horses are finally obtainable in Survival mode.
details: Nautilus — first rideable underwater mob, enables mounted ocean exploration
Spear — new weapon type whose damage scales with the player's movement speed
Zombie Horses — brought into Survival mode as obtainable mounts at last
New underwater combat mechanics — mounted combat works beneath the waves

name: The Copper Age
summary: Massive expansion of copper's usefulness with copper tools, weapons, armor, chests, and even a new copper golem mob.
details: Copper Tools & Weapons — full tool set crafted from copper, offering a tier between stone and iron
Copper Armor — wearable copper armor set that oxidizes over time
Copper Chests — storage blocks with copper aesthetic, oxidize through stages
Copper Golem — new mob that presses copper buttons randomly, oxidizes and freezes over time

name: Chase the Skies
summary: The happy ghast steals the show — find dried ghasts in the Nether, hydrate them into ghastlings, and ride the grown happy ghast to fly around the world. Also adds craftable saddles and Vibrant Visuals.
details: Happy Ghast — a friendly flying mob you can ride with a harness for aerial travel
Dried Ghast — found in the Nether, hydrate it to get a ghastling
Ghastling — baby happy ghast that grows into a rideable happy ghast
Craftable Saddles — saddles can now be crafted instead of only found as loot
Vibrant Visuals — graphical overhaul with improved lighting and shading (Bedrock)
Harness — new item used to ride the happy ghast

name: Spring to Life
summary: Mob variants for warm and cold biomes, updated spawn egg textures, and multiple new flowers and fauna bring the overworld to life.
details: Mob Variants — warm and cold biome variants for existing mobs with unique textures
New Flowers — multiple new flower types added across biomes
Updated Spawn Eggs — all spawn egg textures redesigned
New Fauna — additional ambient creatures bring biomes to life

name: The Garden Awakens
summary: The creaking mob and pale garden biome. Pale oak wood, eyeblossoms, and resin blocks. The creaking heart block spawns creakings at night.
details: Pale Garden — eerie new biome with pale oak trees and eyeblossoms that open at night
The Creaking — hostile mob that can only move when you're not looking at it
Creaking Heart — block that spawns and controls creakings, hidden in pale oak trees
Pale Oak — new full wood set with unique pale color
Resin — new material dropped when creakings are damaged, used for crafting
Eyeblossoms — flowers that open at night and close during the day

name: Bundles of Bravery
summary: Bundles are finally fully released for inventory management. Hardcore mode comes to Bedrock Edition. Minecart improvements and redstone tweaks.
details: Bundles — item storage bags that hold a full stack's worth of mixed items, finally out of experimental
Hardcore Mode on Bedrock — Bedrock Edition finally gets Hardcore mode
Minecart improvements — new physics and stacking behavior
Redstone tweaks — hardcoded changes to piston and observer behavior
New default skins — additional default player models
Gameplay balancing — various mob and combat adjustments

name: Tricky Trials
summary: Trial chambers, the Breeze mob, mace weapon, and new copper and tuff block variants. Ominous trials and vault rewards add a new dungeon-crawling loop.
details: Trial Chambers — procedurally generated underground structures with combat challenges
The Breeze — wind-based hostile mob found in trial chambers, launches wind charges
Mace — new heavy weapon with devastating smash attacks, more damage the further you fall
Trial Spawner — spawns mobs based on nearby player count, rewards on completion
Vault — loot block in trial chambers, each player gets one reward per vault
Ominous Trials — harder variant triggered by Bad Omen, better loot
Wind Charge — throwable projectile that launches entities and interacts with redstone
Copper & Tuff block families — bulbs, grates, doors, trapdoors, chiseled variants
Breeze Rod — crafting material dropped by the Breeze
New banner patterns, pottery sherds, and armor trim templates

name: Armored Paws
summary: The armadillo mob arrives along with armadillo scute for crafting wolf armor. Eight new wolf variants bring diversity to your canine companions.
details: Armadillo — new passive mob that curls into a ball when threatened, drops armadillo scutes
Wolf Armor — craftable from armadillo scutes, protects your tamed wolves
8 Wolf Variants — wolves now have different appearances based on biome
Armadillo Scute — new material dropped by armadillos, used for wolf armor

name: Bats & Pots
summary: Updated bat model with a fresh new look, and decorated pots receive major improvements making them more useful and interactive.
details: Bat Model Update — bats get a completely redesigned model and texture
Decorated Pot Improvements — pots can now store a single item stack, interact with hoppers and comparators
Breeze & Trial Spawner — added behind experimental toggle for upcoming 1.21
Various quality-of-life improvements and bug fixes

name: Trails & Tales
summary: Cherry grove biome, archaeology system with brush tool, armor trims for customization, camels, sniffers, decorated pots, and hanging signs. New bamboo wood set.
details: Cherry Grove — beautiful pink biome with cherry blossom trees and falling petals
Archaeology — brush suspicious sand/gravel to find pottery sherds and rare items
Armor Trims — customize armor with smithing templates and materials for unique looks
Sniffer — ancient mob voted by players, digs up ancient seeds for new plants
Camels — two-player rideable mount that can dash and is too tall for most mobs to reach
Bamboo Wood — full wood set crafted from bamboo, plus bamboo raft
Hanging Signs — decorative signs that hang from blocks
Decorated Pots — craftable from pottery sherds found through archaeology
Calibrated Sculk Sensors — filtered vibration detection for redstone
Chiseled Bookshelf — functional bookshelf that stores up to 6 books

name: The Wild Update
summary: Deep dark biome and ancient cities guarded by the Warden — Minecraft's scariest mob. Mangrove swamps, frogs, tadpoles, allays, and skulk blocks.
details: The Warden — terrifying blind mob that detects vibrations, deals massive damage, basically unkillable
Deep Dark — underground biome covered in sculk, generates ancient cities
Ancient Cities — huge underground structures with loot and sculk shriekers that summon the Warden
Mangrove Swamp — new swamp variant with mangrove trees and mud
Frogs & Tadpoles — three frog variants based on biome temperature, eat small slimes and magma cubes
Allay — friendly mob that collects items matching what you give it, dances to note blocks
Sculk blocks — sensor, shrieker, catalyst, and vein blocks for vibration-based redstone
Mud & Mud Bricks — new building material, made by putting water on dirt
Recovery Compass — points to where you last died
Swift Sneak enchantment — move faster while crouching

name: Caves & Cliffs Part 2
summary: Massive world generation overhaul. World height expanded from 256 to 384 blocks (-64 to 320). Huge caves, dramatic mountains, and new ore distribution.
details: World height expanded — now -64 to 320 (384 total blocks of vertical space)
Completely new terrain generation — more dramatic, varied landscapes
Massive cave systems — cheese caves, spaghetti caves, and noodle caves
Lush Caves — underground biome with azalea, glow berries, dripleaf, and axolotls
Dripstone Caves — stalactites and stalagmites that grow over time
New mountain generation — jagged peaks, frozen peaks, stony peaks, meadows, groves
Ore distribution overhaul — ores generate at different heights, some more common deeper
Deepslate replaces stone below Y=0
Aquifers — underground water and lava lakes at various heights
Noise caves create enormous underground open spaces

name: Caves & Cliffs Part 1
summary: Axolotls, glow squids, goats, copper ore that oxidizes over time, amethyst geodes, spyglass, lightning rods, and deepslate.
details: Axolotls — aquatic mobs that help you fight underwater enemies, come in 5 colors
Glow Squid — drops glow ink sacs for glowing signs and item frames
Goats — mountain mob that rams entities, drops goat horns
Copper — new ore that oxidizes through 4 stages over time, waxable with honeycomb
Amethyst Geodes — underground structures with budding amethyst, grow amethyst clusters
Spyglass — craftable zoom tool using copper and amethyst
Lightning Rod — copper rod that attracts lightning strikes
Deepslate — harder stone variant with full block family
Candles — stackable light source, up to 4 per block
Powder Snow — trap block that entities sink into, leather boots prevent sinking
Glow Berries — food and light source found in lush caves
Tinted Glass — glass that blocks light but you can see through

name: Nether Update
summary: Complete Nether overhaul with four new biomes. Netherite gear surpasses diamond. Piglins, hoglins, striders, and bastion remnants.
details: Crimson Forest — red nether biome with huge fungi, hoglins, and piglins
Warped Forest — blue nether biome, peaceful, with endermen
Soul Sand Valley — vast caverns with soul fire, ghasts, and skeletons
Basalt Deltas — volcanic biome with basalt columns and magma cubes
Netherite — new tier above diamond, found as ancient debris, fire/lava resistant
Piglins — barter with gold, attack if you're not wearing gold armor
Hoglins — hostile nether pigs, breedable, only nether food source
Striders — rideable lava-walking mob
Bastion Remnants — large piglin structures with treasure and pigstep music disc
Ruined Portals — naturally generating broken nether portals in both dimensions
Respawn Anchor — set spawn point in the Nether using glowstone charges
Lodestone — magnetic block that compasses can lock onto

name: Buzzy Bees
summary: Bees, beehives, honey blocks, and honeycomb. Honey blocks have unique sticky/sliding mechanics. Major bug fix update.
details: Bees — pollinate flowers and crops, produce honey, get angry when provoked
Bee Nests — naturally spawn in flower forests, plains, and sunflower plains
Beehives — craftable bee homes
Honey Blocks — sticky blocks with unique movement mechanics, great for redstone
Honeycomb Blocks — decorative building material
Honey Bottles — food item that cures poison
Iron Golem healing — feed iron ingots to heal iron golems
Over 100 bug fixes — massive stability and performance improvements

name: Village & Pillage
summary: Complete villager overhaul with professions and workstations. Pillagers, ravagers, raids, crossbows, and new village structures.
details: Villager rework — professions tied to workstations, better trading, schedules
Pillagers — hostile illager with crossbow, patrol the overworld
Raids — waves of illagers attack villages after you kill a patrol captain
Ravagers — massive illager beast that destroys crops and attacks
Wandering Trader — randomly appears with rare trades
Crossbow — new ranged weapon with multishot and piercing enchantments
Bamboo & Scaffolding — new building/utility blocks
Blast Furnace & Smoker — faster smelting for ores and food
Lectern — hold and display books, redstone output based on page
Loom — simplified banner crafting
New village architecture — unique designs per biome

name: Update Aquatic
summary: Ocean overhaul with coral reefs, kelp forests, sea turtles, dolphins, tridents, conduits, and shipwrecks. Swimming reworked. The Phantom added.
details: Ocean biome variants — warm, lukewarm, cold, frozen, with unique generation
Coral Reefs — colorful underwater structures with 5 coral types
Kelp & Seagrass — underwater vegetation
Sea Turtles — lay eggs on beaches, drop scute for turtle helmet (water breathing)
Dolphins — lead you to shipwrecks and treasure, give speed boost
Drowned — underwater zombie variant, can spawn with tridents
Trident — versatile weapon with 4 unique enchantments (Riptide, Loyalty, Channeling, Impaling)
Conduit — underwater beacon that gives water breathing and attacks mobs
Shipwrecks & Underwater Ruins — loot structures on the ocean floor
Buried Treasure — found with treasure maps from shipwrecks
Phantom — flying undead that attacks if you don't sleep, drops phantom membrane
Swimming mechanic — sprint in water to swim, much faster movement
Heart of the Sea — rare conduit crafting item

name: World of Color
summary: Concrete and glazed terracotta in all 16 colors. Parrots, advancements system, recipe book, and the narrator.
details: Concrete — vibrant solid color blocks, made from concrete powder + water
Glazed Terracotta — 16 patterned decorative blocks with unique designs
Parrots — tameable birds that dance to music and mimic mob sounds
Advancements — replaced achievements with a tree-based progression system
Recipe Book — in-game crafting guide, auto-fills crafting grid
Narrator — accessibility feature that reads chat and UI
Illusioner — unused illager mob that casts blindness and creates duplicates
New colors for beds — beds in all 16 dye colors

name: Exploration Update
summary: Woodland mansions with vindicators and evokers. Shulker boxes for portable storage, totems of undying, llamas, and explorer maps.
details: Woodland Mansions — rare massive structures in dark forests with unique rooms
Vindicators & Evokers — hostile illagers with axes and magic attacks
Vex — small flying hostile mobs summoned by evokers
Shulker Boxes — portable storage that keeps items when broken
Totem of Undying — held item that saves you from death once
Llamas — caravan animals that spit, can be decorated with carpets
Observer — block that detects state changes, outputs redstone pulse
Explorer Maps — bought from cartographers, lead to monuments and mansions
Curse enchantments — Curse of Binding and Curse of Vanishing

name: Frostburn Update
summary: Polar bears, husks, strays, magma blocks, nether wart blocks, bone blocks, and structure blocks.
details: Polar Bears — neutral mob in ice biomes, aggressive when cubs are nearby
Husks — desert zombie variant that inflicts hunger, doesn't burn in sun
Strays — snowy skeleton variant that shoots slowness arrows
Magma Blocks — damage entities standing on them, pull down in water
Nether Wart Block — decorative block crafted from nether wart
Bone Blocks — decorative/structural, found in fossil structures
Structure Blocks — creative/command tool for saving and loading structures
Auto-Jump — controversial new default movement option

name: Combat Update
summary: Dual-wielding, attack cooldowns, shields, elytra wings, end cities, shulkers, and a completely revamped End dimension.
details: Dual-Wielding — offhand slot for shields, torches, maps, food, and more
Attack Cooldown — weapons have a recharge timer, timing matters for full damage
Shields — block attacks, replace sword blocking
Sweep Attack — swords hit multiple mobs in a sweep when fully charged
Elytra — gliding wings found in end ships, revolutionary mobility
End Cities — tall structures in the outer End islands with shulkers and loot
End Ships — floating ships with elytra and dragon head
Shulkers — shell mob that levitates players with projectiles
Chorus Plants & Fruit — End vegetation, fruit teleports you randomly
Dragon's Breath — collectible from Ender Dragon, used for lingering potions
Lingering Potions — splash potions that leave an area effect cloud
End Gateway — portals to outer End islands after killing the dragon
Respawnable Ender Dragon — place end crystals to resummon the fight

name: Bountiful Update
summary: Ocean monuments with guardians, armor stands, banners, slime blocks, new stone types, sea lanterns, and spectator mode.
details: Ocean Monuments — underwater temples guarded by guardians and elder guardians
Guardians — pufferfish-like mobs that shoot lasers
Elder Guardian — mini-boss that inflicts Mining Fatigue
Armor Stands — display armor and items, poseable with commands
Banners — decorative blocks with complex pattern layering system
Slime Blocks — bouncy, sticky blocks, foundation of flying machines
Granite, Diorite, Andesite — three new stone types with polished variants
Sea Lanterns — underwater light source
Prismarine — building blocks from ocean monuments in 3 variants
Spectator Mode — fly through blocks, see through mob eyes
Rabbits — small passive mob with multiple color variants
Depth Strider enchantment — faster underwater movement

name: The Update that Changed the World
summary: Massive biome overhaul adding mesa, mega taiga, roofed forest, savanna, ice spikes, and many more. Stained glass, new flowers, and amplified world type.
details: New biomes — mesa, mega taiga, roofed forest, birch forest, savanna, sunflower plains, ice plains spikes, deep ocean, stone beach, flower forest, and more
Stained Glass — glass in all 16 colors, plus stained glass panes
New flowers — 10 new flower types including sunflowers, lilacs, peonies, rose bushes
Packed Ice — non-melting ice block found in ice spikes biome
Podzol — dirt variant found in mega taiga
Red Sand — found in mesa biomes
Amplified world type — exaggerated terrain for dramatic landscapes
New fishing mechanics — treasure and junk loot tables
Acacia & Dark Oak — two new wood types with full block sets
Command blocks rewrite — more powerful command system

name: Horse Update
summary: Horses, donkeys, mules with rideable mechanics and armor. Leads, hay bales, carpets, hardened clay, and resource packs.
details: Horses — tameable, rideable, breedable with variable speed/jump/health stats
Donkeys & Mules — chest-carrying variants of horses
Horse Armor — iron, gold, and diamond armor for horses
Leads — rope to tie animals to fences or lead them around
Hay Bales — decorative and food for horses
Carpets — thin wool decoration for floors and llamas
Hardened Clay & Stained Clay — terracotta building material in 16 colors
Coal Blocks — compact coal storage, good fuel source
Resource Packs — replaced texture packs, can include sounds and models

name: Redstone Update
summary: Redstone overhaul with hoppers, droppers, comparators, daylight sensors, trapped chests, and quartz blocks.
details: Hopper — transfers items between containers, essential for automation
Dropper — ejects items or feeds them into containers
Comparator — reads container fullness, signal strength, and compares signals
Daylight Sensor — outputs redstone based on time of day
Trapped Chest — outputs redstone signal when opened
Weighted Pressure Plates — gold and iron, output based on entity count
Block of Redstone — compact redstone storage, acts as power source
Nether Quartz — ore found in the Nether, used for quartz blocks and comparators
Quartz blocks — pillar, chiseled, and smooth variants
Minecart with Hopper & TNT — new minecart variants

name: Pretty Scary Update
summary: Witches, the Wither boss, bats, beacons, anvils, command blocks, and new food items. Night vision and invisibility potions.
details: The Wither — second boss mob, summoned by placing soul sand and wither skulls
Beacons — powerful buff blocks powered by mineral pyramids
Witches — hostile mob that throws harmful potions and drinks healing potions
Wither Skeletons — nether fortress mob that drops skulls for Wither summoning
Anvil — repair and rename items, combine enchantments
Command Blocks — programmable blocks for map makers and servers
Bats — ambient cave mob
Carrots, Potatoes, Pumpkin Pie — new food items
Item Frames — display items on walls
Flower Pots — place flowers and saplings as decoration
Cobblestone Walls — fence-like walls from cobblestone and mossy cobblestone
Night Vision & Invisibility potions — new potion effects

name: The Adventure Update II
summary: Trading with villagers, emeralds, desert and jungle temples, ender chests, tripwire hooks, written books, and adventure mode.
details: Villager Trading — buy and sell items with villagers using emeralds
Emeralds — new currency ore found in mountains
Desert Temples — loot structure with TNT trap
Jungle Temples — loot structure with arrow trap and puzzle
Ender Chests — shared storage accessible from any ender chest
Tripwire Hooks — redstone trap component using string
Written Books — sign books and share written content
Adventure Mode — can't break blocks without the right tool
Singleplayer runs on internal server — foundation for LAN play

name: The Jungle Update
summary: Jungle biome with ocelots and cocoa beans. World height doubled to 256. Iron golems, redstone lamps, and upside-down stairs.
details: Jungle Biome — dense forest with tall trees, vines, and temple structures
Ocelots — tameable jungle cats that scare creepers
Cocoa Beans — farmable on jungle logs
World Height doubled — from 128 to 256 blocks
Iron Golems — village protectors, craftable from iron blocks and pumpkin
Redstone Lamps — toggleable light source
Upside-Down Stairs & Slabs — place on top of blocks
Fire Charge — fireball item, used as flint and steel alternative
Bottle o' Enchanting — throwable XP in survival

summary: Superflat world type, spawn eggs in creative, bow enchantments, sheep regrow wool, and language translations.
details: Superflat world type — flat terrain for creative building
Spawn Eggs — spawn mobs in creative mode
Bow enchantments — Power, Punch, Flame, Infinity
Sheep regrow wool — sheep eat grass to regrow sheared wool
56 language translations — Minecraft goes global
Beach biome improvements — better generation

name: The Official Release
summary: Minecraft officially leaves beta. The End dimension, Ender Dragon boss fight, enchanting, brewing, XP system, hardcore mode, and The End poem.
details: The End — new dimension accessed through stronghold End Portals
Ender Dragon — first boss fight in Minecraft, credits roll on defeat
The End Poem — philosophical text displayed after defeating the dragon
Enchanting — spend XP at enchanting tables for gear buffs
Brewing — craft potions at brewing stands using nether wart and ingredients
XP System — experience orbs from mobs, mining, smelting
Hardcore Mode — one life, world deletes on death
Villagers — passive NPC mobs in villages (trading came in 1.3)
Snow Golems — craftable snowball-throwing mob
Mooshrooms — mushroom-covered cows on mushroom islands
Baby Animals — passive mobs can breed and have babies
Nether Fortress — structure with blaze spawners and nether wart
